use_codes.desc: GENERAL USE CLAIM SUBMITTED FOR 12 NEXIUM PATIENTS STATING 'PERTINENT TO THE CAPSULE FORMULATION FOR NEXIUM AND ITS INDICATIONS FOR THE TREATMENT OF GERD AND ERADICATION OF H.PYLORI TO REDUCE THE RISK OF DUODENAL ULCER RECURRENCE
TREATMENT OF GASTROESOPHAGEAL REFLEX DISEASE (GERD) AND ERADICATION OF H.PYLORI TO REDUCE RISK OF DUODENAL ULCER RECURRENCE
TREATMENT OF GERD, MAINTENANCE OF HEALING OF EROSIVE ESOPHAGITIS AND RISK REDUCTION OF NSAID ASSOCIATED GASTRIC ULCERS
TREATMENT OF GASTROESOPHAGEAL REFLUX DISEASE (GERD), RISK-REDUCTION OF NSAID-ASSOCIATED GASTRIC ULCER, H. PYLORI ERADICATION TO REDUCE THE RISK OF DUODENAL ULCER RECURRENCE
LONG-TERM TREATMENT OF PATHOLOGICAL HYPERSECRETORY CONDITIONS
USE FOR TREATMENT OF HELICOBACTER INFECTIONS

indications.text:
1.1 Treatment of Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD)
Healing of Erosive Esophagitis
NEXIUM is indicated for the short-term treatment (4 to 8 weeks) in the healing and
symptomatic resolution of diagnostically confirmed erosive esophagitis. For those patients
who have not healed after 4 to 8 weeks ...

1.1 Healing of Erosive Esophagitis (EE)
Adults
NEXIUM delayed-release capsules and NEXIUM for delayed-release oral suspension are indicated for the short-term
treatment (4 to 8 weeks) in the healing and symptomatic resolution of diagnostically confirmed EE in adults. For those
patients who have not ...
